resultRaw.sql:
SELECT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM hire_date)::int AS hire_year, COUNT(id) AS professors_hired
FROM public.professors
WHERE hire_date IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY EXTRACT(YEAR FROM hire_date)::int
ORDER BY hire_year ASC;

issues: x-axis field 'hire_year' is encoded as type 'ordinal'; since this is a time series (year), use a temporal or quantitative type so axis formatting/ticks and scale reflect time.
Tooltip and x encoding both use ordinal for hire_year; change to temporal/quantitative to get correct year formatting and interactions (e.g., continuous timeline behavior).
Using 'sort':'ascending' on an ordinal year field may produce correct order, but relying on a temporal/quantitative axis is more robust for chronological sorting and labeling.

result.sql:
SELECT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM hire_date)::int AS hire_year, COUNT(id) AS professors_hired
FROM public.professors
WHERE hire_date IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY EXTRACT(YEAR FROM hire_date)::int
ORDER BY hire_year ASC;

issues: x-axis field 'hire_year' is encoded as type 'ordinal'; since this is a time series (year), use a temporal or quantitative type so axis formatting/ticks and scale reflect time.
Tooltip and x encoding both use ordinal for hire_year; change to temporal/quantitative to get correct year formatting and interactions (e.g., continuous timeline behavior).
Using 'sort':'ascending' on an ordinal year field may produce correct order, but relying on a temporal/quantitative axis is more robust for chronological sorting and labeling.
